KSI-AFR-01.description: Validates the complete FedRAMP Minimum Assessment Scope (MAS) covering machine resources, human resources, and process resources. This rule enforces Structural Boundaries (VPC ID) for compute assets, validates peripheral assets (S3, RDS, ALB), enumerates privileged human access (IAM), and confirms governance processes exist.
KSI-AFR-01.justification: Validates the Live FedRAMP Boundary (MAS) across all three resource types: 1) Machine resources — the four critical asset classes (VPC boundary, EC2 compute, RDS data, S3 storage, ALB network), 2) Human resources — privileged IAM users/roles with production access, 3) Process resources — governance documentation defining security procedures.

cli_commands.note: BOUNDARY: Validate the dedicated Production VPC (The Structural Boundary of the MAS).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least one non-default VPC must exist as the production boundary

cli_commands.note: COMPUTE: Validate that all active EC2 instances reside within the defined VPC Boundary.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least one running EC2 instance must exist within the boundary

cli_commands.command: aws rds describe-db-instances --query "DBInstances[*].{ID:DBInstanceIdentifier, Engine:Engine, VPC:DBSubnetGroup.VpcId}" --output json
cli_commands.note: DATABASE: Validate RDS instances to ensure federal data is contained within the boundary.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 RDS instance must exist

cli_commands.note: STORAGE: Validate S3 buckets (Peripheral Assets) that fall within the assessment scope.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 S3 bucket must exist

cli_commands.command: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query "LoadBalancers[*].{ARN:LoadBalancerArn, DNS:DNSName, VPC:VpcId}" --output json
cli_commands.note: NETWORK: Validate Load Balancers (The Ingress/Egress points of the boundary).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 load balancer must exist

cli_commands.command: aws iam list-users --query "Users[*].{UserName:UserName, CreateDate:CreateDate, PasswordLastUsed:PasswordLastUsed}" --output json
cli_commands.note: HUMAN: Enumerate IAM users with production account access (privileged human resources in the MAS).
cli_commands.expected_output: IAM user list establishes the human resource inventory for the boundary

cli_commands.command: aws iam list-roles --query "Roles[?contains(RoleName, 'Admin') || contains(RoleName, 'Security') || contains(RoleName, 'Incident')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: HUMAN-ROLES: Enumerate security-relevant IAM roles (ISSO, SysAdmin, IR roles) mapped to human resource responsibilities.
cli_commands.expected_output: Security-relevant IAM roles must exist mapping to ISSO, SysAdmin, and IR responsibilities

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-folder --repository-name security-governance --folder-path procedures/ --output json
cli_commands.note: PROCESS: Validate that security governance procedures exist (process resources in the MAS).
cli_commands.expected_output: The procedures folder must exist in CodeCommit with security governance documentation

KSI-AFR-01.implementation_summary: Meridian maintains a single-VPC architecture with all production resources (5 EC2 instances, 1 RDS database, 11 S3 buckets, 1 ALB) explicitly enumerated and validated daily. Human resources are inventoried via IAM user and role enumeration, with security-relevant roles (Admin, Security, Incident Response) specifically tracked. Process resources are validated by confirming the existence of governance procedures in the security-governance CodeCommit repository. Our small footprint enables complete boundary visibility across machine, human, and process resources.
KSI-AFR-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: A dedicated non-default VPC exists as the production boundary, at least one running EC2 instance operates within it, at least one RDS database instance is provisioned, S3 buckets exist for storage, at least one load balancer provides ingress/egress, IAM users and security-relevant roles are enumerated, and security governance procedures exist in CodeCommit.
KSI-AFR-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No non-default VPC exists, no running EC2 instances are found, no RDS instances are provisioned, no S3 buckets exist, no load balancers are configured, IAM enumeration fails, or security governance procedures are missing. Any missing resource class (machine, human, or process) means the Minimum Assessment Scope is incomplete.

KSI-AFR-02.description: Set security goals and develop automated validation of status and progress.
KSI-AFR-02.justification: Validates the Active Compliance Tracker. Checks for the 'unified_ksi_validations.json' artifact in the S3 compliance bucket. This file represents the aggregated status of all Phase 2 KSIs, proving that security goals are actively tracked and validated.

cli_commands.command: aws s3api head-object --bucket fedramp-compliance-893894210484 --key data/unified_ksi_validations.json --output json
cli_commands.note: TRACKER: Validate existence and freshness of the Master Compliance Scorecard (unified_ksi_validations.json).
cli_commands.expected_output: Compliance scorecard must exist, be non-empty, and updated within 30 days (proving active tracking)

cli_commands.note: SOURCE: Validate Security Hub is enabled (The raw data source feeding the tracker).
cli_commands.expected_output: Security Hub must be enabled

KSI-AFR-02.implementation_summary: Security goals are tracked via unified_ksi_validations.json, auto-generated by GitHub Actions and published to our Trust Center API. All 61 KSIs are evaluated daily with results publicly accessible.
KSI-AFR-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: The unified_ksi_validations.json compliance scorecard exists in the S3 compliance bucket with a LastModified timestamp within the last 7 days, AND AWS Security Hub is enabled and returning a valid HubArn.
KSI-AFR-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The compliance scorecard artifact is missing from S3, its LastModified date is older than 7 days (stale data), or Security Hub is not enabled in the account.

KSI-AFR-03.description: Determine how authorization data will be shared in alignment with the ADS standard.
KSI-AFR-03.justification: Validates the ADS Deliverable. Checks specifically for the 'meridian-authorization-package-LATEST.zip' file. This confirms that the automated pipeline has successfully compiled the required documents into a distributable format.

cli_commands.note: DELIVERABLE: Validate the existence of the downloadable Authorization Package ZIP file.
cli_commands.expected_output: S3 artifact must exist

cli_commands.note: INTERFACE: Ping the Trust Center API to ensure the delivery mechanism is live.
cli_commands.expected_output: HTTP 200 status code expected from Trust Center API

KSI-AFR-03.implementation_summary: Authorization data is shared via our public Trust Center (trust.meridianks.com) and API. The authorization package ZIP is auto-compiled from CodeCommit governance artifacts and updated with each pipeline run.
KSI-AFR-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: The meridian-authorization-package-LATEST.zip artifact exists in the S3 compliance bucket, AND the Trust Center API endpoint returns an HTTP 200 status code.
KSI-AFR-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The authorization package ZIP is missing from S3, or the Trust Center API returns a non-200 status code (service unavailable).

KSI-AFR-04.description: Document the vulnerability detection and response methodology.
KSI-AFR-04.justification: Validates VDR Outputs. Instead of just checking if scanners are 'on', this validates the 'assessed_vulnerabilities_integrated.json' and 'vdr_vulnerability_status.json' artifacts. These prove that vulnerability data is being actively collected, integrated, and tracked.

cli_commands.note: DETECTION: Validate the Integrated Vulnerability Report artifact.
cli_commands.expected_output: Vulnerability report must exist, be non-empty, and updated within 30 days (proving active detection)

cli_commands.command: aws s3api head-object --bucket fedramp-compliance-893894210484 --key data/vdr_vulnerability_status.json --output json
cli_commands.note: STATUS: Validate the VDR Status artifact (Proof of remediation tracking).
cli_commands.expected_output: VDR status artifact must exist, be non-empty, and updated within 30 days (proving remediation tracking)

cli_commands.note: METHODOLOGY: Validate the Governance Policy defining the VDR process.
cli_commands.expected_output: VDR governance policy must exist with substantive content defining the vulnerability management methodology

KSI-AFR-04.implementation_summary: Vulnerability detection uses AWS Inspector and SecurityHub with VDR Release 25.09A compliance. Findings are aggregated into assessed_vulnerabilities_integrated.json with automated remediation tracking. Current posture: 0 CISA KEV matches, 0 internet-reachable criticals.
KSI-AFR-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: Both vulnerability artifacts (assessed_vulnerabilities_integrated.json and vdr_vulnerability_status.json) exist in S3, AND the vulnerability-management-policy.md governance document exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-AFR-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Either vulnerability artifact is missing from S3, or the vulnerability management policy document does not exist in the security-governance repository.

KSI-AFR-05.description: Verify SCN procedures are documented and active change tracking is maintained.
KSI-AFR-05.justification: Validates SCN Evidence in the 'Audit Drop Box' bucket.

cli_commands.note: DROP BOX: Validate existence of the Compliance Evidence bucket.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 compliance/evidence S3 bucket must exist

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-objects-v2 --bucket $(aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[?contains(Name, 'compliance') || contains(Name, 'evidence')].Name" --output text | head -n 1) --prefix scn-flow-validation.html --output json
cli_commands.note: PROCEDURE: Check for the 'scn-flow-validation.html' file.
cli_commands.expected_output: SCN flow validation artifact must exist and be non-empty (proving the procedure is documented)

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-objects-v2 --bucket $(aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[?contains(Name, 'compliance') || contains(Name, 'evidence')].Name" --output text | head -n 1) --prefix audit_trail --output json
cli_commands.note: LOGS: Check for the SCN Audit Trail file.
cli_commands.expected_output: SCN audit trail must exist with recent entries (proving changes are actively tracked)

KSI-AFR-05.implementation_summary: Significant Change Notifications are tracked via CodeCommit commit history and Terraform state versioning. All infrastructure changes require PR approval and generate immutable audit trails.
KSI-AFR-05.pass_criteria: PASS if: A compliance/evidence S3 bucket exists, the scn-flow-validation.html procedure document is present in it, AND an audit_trail prefix contains SCN log entries.
KSI-AFR-05.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No compliance/evidence bucket exists, the SCN flow validation HTML is missing, or no audit trail entries are found.

KSI-AFR-06.description: Maintain a plan and process for providing Ongoing Authorization Reports and Quarterly Reviews.
KSI-AFR-06.justification: Validates CCM Evidence. Checks for the '3pao_audit_report.json' artifact. This validates that the continuous monitoring process includes independent assessment data, supporting the 'Collaborative' aspect of the FedRAMP CCM standard.

cli_commands.command: aws s3api head-object --bucket fedramp-compliance-893894210484 --key data/3pao_audit_report.json --output json
cli_commands.note: INDEPENDENT REVIEW: Validate the 3PAO Audit Report artifact.
cli_commands.expected_output: S3 artifact must exist

cli_commands.note: AVAILABILITY: Ping the Trust Center Portal (The delivery vehicle for these reports).
cli_commands.expected_output: HTTP 200 status code expected from Trust Center portal

KSI-AFR-06.implementation_summary: Ongoing authorization reports are auto-generated and published to our Trust Center. 3PAO audit reports are stored in the compliance bucket with quarterly review cadence built into GitHub Actions workflows.
KSI-AFR-06.pass_criteria: PASS if: The 3pao_audit_report.json artifact exists in the S3 compliance bucket, AND the Trust Center portal returns an HTTP 200 status code confirming public availability of reports.
KSI-AFR-06.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The 3PAO audit report artifact is missing from S3, or the Trust Center portal is unreachable (non-200 response).

KSI-AFR-07.description: Document the secure configuration baseline for the cloud service offering.
KSI-AFR-07.justification: Validates the Secure Configuration Baseline via API. Instead of checking a static file, this queries the live '/prod/config' endpoint, proving that the security baseline is actively published and available to consumers.

cli_commands.note: DYNAMIC RETRIEVAL: Fetch the Security Baseline from the live API. This proves the configuration standards are documented and programmatically accessible.
cli_commands.expected_output: Non-empty response expected

KSI-AFR-07.implementation_summary: Security configuration baseline is documented in CodeCommit (security-governance repo) and served via our /prod/config API endpoint. Baselines cover EC2 hardening, RDS encryption, S3 policies, and network segmentation.
KSI-AFR-07.pass_criteria: PASS if: The /prod/config API endpoint returns a non-empty JSON response containing the security configuration baseline with defined standards for compute, database, storage, and network resources.
KSI-AFR-07.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The API endpoint returns an empty response, an error, or does not include security baseline configuration data.

KSI-AFR-08.description: Operate a secure inbox to receive critical communication from FedRAMP and other government entities.
KSI-AFR-08.justification: Validates FSI Operations. Checks for: 1) The Security Inbox Procedure Document (Governance), and 2) The SNS Topic used to aggregate and forward internal alerts to that inbox (Technical infrastructure).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path procedures/security-inbox-procedures.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the existence of the Security Inbox Procedures document.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.command: aws sns list-topics --query "Topics[?contains(TopicArn, 'security') || contains(TopicArn, 'alert') || contains(TopicArn, 'admin')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: INFRASTRUCTURE: Validate existence of an SNS Topic used to forward AWS alerts to the security inbox.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 SNS topic must exist

KSI-AFR-08.implementation_summary: FedRAMP Secure Inbox operates via SNS topics with email forwarding to security@meridianks.com. Alerts from GuardDuty, SecurityHub, and CloudWatch route through dedicated security topics with <1hr SLA.
KSI-AFR-08.pass_criteria: PASS if: The security-inbox-procedures.md document exists in CodeCommit, AND at least one SNS topic with 'security', 'alert', or 'admin' in the ARN exists for alert forwarding.
KSI-AFR-08.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The security inbox procedures document is missing from CodeCommit, or no SNS topics exist for security alert forwarding.

KSI-AFR-09.description: Persistently validate security posture using automated pipelines.
KSI-AFR-09.justification: Validates Automated Pipeline Artifacts. Checks for: 1) The Validation Architecture Diagram (Governance), and 2) The 'unified_ksi_validations.json' artifact in the S3 'data/' folder. This proves the Master Validator pipeline is successfully executing and actively refreshing the compliance data served by the Trust Center.

cli_commands.note: ARCHITECTURE: Validate the existence of the Validation Architecture Diagram.
cli_commands.expected_output: Validation architecture document must exist with substantive content (sections, diagrams)

cli_commands.command: aws s3api head-object --bucket fedramp-compliance-893894210484 --key data/unified_ksi_validations.json --output json
cli_commands.note: EXECUTION: Validate existence (and freshness) of the Master Validation artifact in the production compliance bucket.
cli_commands.expected_output: Master validation artifact must exist, be non-empty, and updated within 30 days (proving persistent validation)

KSI-AFR-09.implementation_summary: Automated validation runs via GitHub Actions (master-validator.yml) with 76% CI success rate. Pipeline validates all 61 KSIs, generates compliance artifacts, and publishes to Trust Center. Execution logs retained 90+ days.
KSI-AFR-09.pass_criteria: PASS if: The validation architecture diagram (fedramp-phase2-validation-architecture.mmd) exists in CodeCommit, AND the unified_ksi_validations.json artifact exists in the S3 compliance bucket with a recent LastModified timestamp.
KSI-AFR-09.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The validation architecture diagram is missing from CodeCommit, or the master validation artifact is absent or stale in S3.

KSI-AFR-10.description: Integrate FedRAMP's Incident Communications Procedures (ICP) into incident response procedures and infrastructure.
KSI-AFR-10.justification: Validates ICP Readiness. Checks for: 1) The ICP Playbook and IR Plan (Governance), and 2) Dedicated Security SNS Topics (Infrastructure). Filtering the SNS topics specifically for 'security', 'incident', or 'alert' proves that a dedicated communication channel exists for high-priority events.

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path procedures/icp_playbook.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the FedRAMP Incident Communications Procedure (ICP) Playbook.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path procedures/incident-response-plan.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the master Incident Response Plan.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.command: aws sns list-topics --query "Topics[?contains(TopicArn, 'security') || contains(TopicArn, 'incident') || contains(TopicArn, 'alert')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: INFRASTRUCTURE: Validate existence of dedicated SNS topics for Security Incidents.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 SNS topic must exist

cli_commands.command: aws sns list-subscriptions --query "Subscriptions[?contains(TopicArn, 'security') || contains(TopicArn, 'incident') || contains(TopicArn, 'alert')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: REACHABILITY: Validate that the security topics have active subscriptions (email/SMS/Lambda) to ensure alerts are actually delivered.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 active subscription must exist on security SNS topics

KSI-AFR-10.implementation_summary: ICP integration uses GitHub Actions for automated FedRAMP/CISA notification within 1 hour of incident detection. Playbooks in CodeCommit define escalation paths for Critical, High, and standard incidents.
KSI-AFR-10.pass_criteria: PASS if: Both the ICP playbook and incident response plan exist in CodeCommit, at least one dedicated security/incident/alert SNS topic exists, AND those topics have active subscriptions (email, SMS, or Lambda) to ensure alert delivery.
KSI-AFR-10.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Either governance document (ICP playbook or IR plan) is missing, no dedicated security SNS topics exist, or the topics have zero active subscriptions (alerts would not be delivered).

KSI-AFR-11.description: Ensure that cryptographic modules are selected and used in alignment with the FedRAMP 20x Using Cryptographic Modules (UCM) policy.
KSI-AFR-11.justification: Validates Applied Cryptography. Checks for: 1) Active KMS Keys (Key Management), 2) S3 Encryption on the Compliance Bucket (Data at Rest), 3) RDS Encryption (Data at Rest), and 4) Active Load Balancer Listeners enforcing TLS 1.2+ (Data in Transit/FIPS alignment).

cli_commands.note: KEY MANAGEMENT: Validate that KMS keys exist and are available for cryptographic operations.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 KMS key must exist

cli_commands.note: DATA AT REST (STORAGE): Validate that the Compliance Artifact Bucket enforces server-side encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS).
cli_commands.expected_output: Encryption configuration must exist

cli_commands.note: DATA AT REST (DATABASE): Validate that all RDS instances have 'StorageEncrypted' set to true.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 RDS database instance must exist

cli_commands.command: aws elbv2 describe-listeners --load-balancer-arn $(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query "LoadBalancers[0].LoadBalancerArn" --output text) --output json || echo "[]"
cli_commands.note: DATA IN TRANSIT: Validate that Load Balancer Listeners are configured with secure SSL Policies (TLS 1.2+).
cli_commands.expected_output: Load balancer listeners must use TLS 1.2+ SSL policies (e.g., ELBSecurityPolicy-TLS-1-2-2017-01 or newer)

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/cryptographic-modules-policy.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the existence of the Cryptographic Modules (UCM) Policy.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-AFR-11.implementation_summary: Cryptography uses AWS KMS with AES-256 for data at rest (S3, RDS, EBS). TLS 1.2+ enforced on ALB listeners. FIPS 140-2 validated modules via AWS GovCloud-compatible services.
KSI-AFR-11.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one KMS key exists for cryptographic operations, the compliance S3 bucket enforces server-side encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS), all RDS instances have StorageEncrypted=true, load balancer listeners enforce TLS 1.2+ via secure SSL policies, AND the cryptographic-modules-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-AFR-11.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No KMS keys exist, the compliance bucket lacks encryption configuration, any RDS instance has StorageEncrypted=false, load balancer listeners allow TLS below 1.2 or use insecure SSL policies, or the UCM policy document is missing.

KSI-CED-01.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of training given to all employees on policies, procedures, and security-related topics.
KSI-CED-01.justification: Validates 'GitOps' Security Training. Instead of passive LMS logs, this validates the 'training/register.json' in the governance repo. This proves staff have actively attested to security training by decoding a challenge token (CTF style) and committing it to the immutable version control history.

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path training/register.json --output json
cli_commands.note: EVIDENCE: Validate the Training Register. The file must contain user commits with the correct decoded flag, proving active engagement.
cli_commands.expected_output: Training register must exist and contain attestation entries (user/token/date records proving completion)

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path training/security-refresh.md --output json
cli_commands.note: CURRICULUM: Validate the existence of the Training Challenge Instructions (The 'Lesson Plan').
cli_commands.expected_output: Training curriculum must exist with substantive content (structured training material, not an empty shell)

KSI-CED-01.implementation_summary: Training tracked via GitOps - employees commit signed attestations to training/register.json after completing security awareness modules. CTF-style challenge tokens prove active engagement.
KSI-CED-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: The training/register.json file exists in CodeCommit containing at least one signed attestation entry with a decoded challenge token, AND the training/security-refresh.md curriculum document exists.
KSI-CED-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The training register is missing or contains zero attestation entries, or the training curriculum document does not exist in CodeCommit.

KSI-CED-02.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of role-specific training given to employees in high risk roles, including at least roles with privileged access.
KSI-CED-02.justification: Validates 'GitOps' Privileged User Training. Checks the 'training/privileged_register.json' file, which serves as a segregated training record for high-risk roles. Presence of signed commits here proves that privileged users have completed the 'Deep Dive with Security: IAM' module and attested to the responsibilities defined in the Privileged Access Guide.

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path training/privileged_register.json --output json
cli_commands.note: EVIDENCE: Validate the Privileged User Training Register. Entries here confirm acknowledgement of high-risk responsibilities and completion of the IAM Deep Dive.
cli_commands.expected_output: Privileged user training register must contain attestation entries proving role-specific training completion

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path training/privileged-access-guide.md --output json
cli_commands.note: CURRICULUM: Validate the existence of the Privileged Access Guide containing the link to the mandatory AWS Skill Builder IAM course.
cli_commands.expected_output: Privileged access training guide must exist with substantive content covering role-specific security topics

KSI-CED-02.implementation_summary: Privileged user training requires completion of AWS Skill Builder IAM Deep Dive course. Attestations stored in training/privileged_register.json with commit signatures.
KSI-CED-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: The training/privileged_register.json file exists in CodeCommit with signed attestations from privileged users confirming IAM Deep Dive completion, AND the privileged-access-guide.md exists with the mandatory AWS Skill Builder course link.
KSI-CED-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The privileged user training register is missing or empty, or the privileged access guide document does not exist in CodeCommit.

KSI-CED-03.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of role-specific training given to development and engineering staff that covers best practices for delivering secure software.
KSI-CED-03.justification: Validates 'GitOps' Developer Security Training. Checks the 'training/developer_register.json' file. This proves engineering staff have reviewed the existing 'secure-software-development.md' policy and attested to understanding secure delivery practices via version control.

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path training/developer_register.json --output json
cli_commands.note: EVIDENCE: Validate the Developer Training Register. Entries here confirm engineering staff have acknowledged the secure software development policy.
cli_commands.expected_output: Developer training register must contain attestation entries proving secure development training completion

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/secure-software-development.md --output json
cli_commands.note: CURRICULUM: Validate the existence of the Secure Software Development policy.
cli_commands.expected_output: Secure software development policy must exist with substantive content covering SDLC security practices

KSI-CED-03.implementation_summary: Developer security training covers OWASP Top 10, secure coding in Python/TypeScript, and Checkov IaC scanning. Attestations in training/developer_register.json.
KSI-CED-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: The training/developer_register.json file exists in CodeCommit with signed attestations from engineering staff, AND the secure-software-development.md policy exists in the policies directory.
KSI-CED-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The developer training register is missing or contains no attestations, or the secure software development policy document is absent.

KSI-CED-04.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of role-specific training given to staff involved with incident response or disaster recovery.
KSI-CED-04.justification: Validates 'GitOps' IR/DR Training. Checks the 'training/ir_dr_register.json' file. This proves that designated Incident Responders have reviewed the Incident Response Plan and attested to their readiness via version control commits, satisfying the requirement for role-specific IR/DR training effectiveness monitoring.

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path training/ir_dr_register.json --output json
cli_commands.note: EVIDENCE: Validate the IR/DR Training Register. Entries here confirm the IR team has reviewed the active response procedures.
cli_commands.expected_output: IR/DR training register must contain attestation entries proving incident response training completion

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path procedures/incident-response-plan.md --output json
cli_commands.note: CURRICULUM: Validate the existence of the Incident Response Plan (The core training material).
cli_commands.expected_output: Incident response plan must exist with substantive content covering response procedures and escalation

KSI-CED-04.implementation_summary: IR/DR training requires review of incident-response-plan.md and participation in tabletop exercises. Attestations tracked in training/ir_dr_register.json.
KSI-CED-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: The training/ir_dr_register.json file exists in CodeCommit with attestations from designated incident responders, AND the incident-response-plan.md exists as the core training material.
KSI-CED-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The IR/DR training register is missing or empty, or the incident response plan document does not exist in CodeCommit.

KSI-CMT-01.description: Log and monitor modifications to the cloud service offering.
KSI-CMT-01.justification: Validates Change Logging. Checks for: 1) Active CloudTrail (Logs the API calls/modifications), 2) Active AWS Config Recorder (Logs the resource state changes), and 3) The Change Management Policy.

cli_commands.note: AUDIT: Validate existence of CloudTrail trails (The primary change logger).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CloudTrail trail must exist

cli_commands.command: aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name $(aws cloudtrail describe-trails --query 'trailList[0].TrailARN' --output text) --output json
cli_commands.note: SCOPE: Validate that the trail is recording 'Management Events' (Read/Write API calls).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 event selector must exist

cli_commands.note: TRACKING: Validate that AWS Config is actively recording resource changes (Recording: true).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Config recorder must exist

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/change-management-policy.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the Change Management Policy document.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-CMT-01.implementation_summary: All modifications logged via CloudTrail (management events) and AWS Config (resource state). Change management policy requires Terraform-only changes with PR approval.
KSI-CMT-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one CloudTrail trail exists and is logging, event selectors are configured to record Management Events (Read/Write API calls), AWS Config recorder is actively recording (recording=true), AND the change-management-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-CMT-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No CloudTrail trail exists, no event selectors are configured, the Config recorder is not recording, or the change management policy is missing.

KSI-CMT-02.description: Execute changes to machine-based information resources through redeployment of version controlled immutable resources rather than direct modification wherever possible.
KSI-CMT-02.justification: Validates Infrastructure as Code (IaC). Since ASGs are not used, this validates that changes are managed via Terraform State (Version Control) and State Locking (Concurrency Control), ensuring changes are tracked in code rather than manual console actions.

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[?contains(Name, 'terraform') || contains(Name, 'state') || contains(Name, 'tfstate')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: VERSION CONTROL: Validate existence of the Terraform State bucket (Proof that infrastructure is defined as code).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 S3 bucket with 'terraform', 'state', or 'tfstate' in the name must exist

cli_commands.command: aws dynamodb list-tables --query "TableNames[?contains(@, 'terraform') || contains(@, 'lock') || contains(@, 'state')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: MANAGEMENT: Validate existence of the Terraform Lock Table (Proof of active, safe IaC workflows).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 DynamoDB table with 'terraform', 'lock', or 'state' in the name must exist

cli_commands.note: INVENTORY: List the active EC2 instances managed by this Terraform state.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 EC2 instance must exist

cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the policy that mandates Terraform usage for all production changes.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-CMT-02.implementation_summary: Infrastructure deployed exclusively via Terraform with state stored in S3 (mks-states bucket) and DynamoDB locking. No console changes permitted in production.
KSI-CMT-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: A Terraform state S3 bucket exists (containing 'terraform', 'state', or 'tfstate' in the name), a DynamoDB lock table exists for state locking, at least one running EC2 instance is managed by Terraform, AND the configuration-management-policy.md mandating IaC exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-CMT-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No Terraform state bucket exists (infrastructure not managed as code), no DynamoDB lock table exists (no concurrency control), no managed EC2 instances exist, or the IaC policy document is missing.

KSI-CMT-03.description: Automate persistent testing and validation of changes throughout deployment.
KSI-CMT-03.justification: Validates the Hybrid Compliance Strategy. Checks for: 1) Active AWS Config Recorder (The Persistent Monitor), 2) Active Config Rules (The Validation Logic), and 3) The Testing Policy (Governance of the CI/CD pipeline).

cli_commands.note: MONITOR: Validate that the Configuration Recorder is recording (The engine for persistent validation).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Config recorder must exist

cli_commands.note: VALIDATION: Validate existence of active Config Rules that enforce security policies on live resources.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 active AWS Config rule must exist

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/sdlc-policy.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the SDLC Policy which mandates 'Automated Testing' (Checkov) before deployment.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-CMT-03.implementation_summary: Changes validated by Checkov scans in CI pipeline before deployment. AWS Config rules provide continuous drift detection post-deployment.
KSI-CMT-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: AWS Config recorder is actively recording, at least one active Config Rule exists enforcing security policies on live resources, AND the sdlc-policy.md mandating automated testing (Checkov) exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-CMT-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Config recorder is not active, no Config Rules are in ACTIVE state, or the SDLC policy document is missing from CodeCommit.

KSI-CMT-04.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of documented change management procedures.
KSI-CMT-04.justification: Validates Adherence to Procedure via State History. Checks for: 1) The Change Management Procedure Document (Governance) and 2) Terraform State File Version History (Technical proof of sequential, tracked changes to infrastructure).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path procedures/change-management-procedure.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the existence of the Change Management Procedure document.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-objects-v2 --bucket mks-states --prefix terraform.tfstate --max-items 1 --output json
cli_commands.note: IMMUTABILITY: List Terraform State file with versioning metadata, proving version-controlled infrastructure changes.
cli_commands.expected_output: S3 object must exist at specified prefix

KSI-CMT-04.implementation_summary: Change management effectiveness measured via Terraform state version history and CloudTrail event analysis. All changes traceable to specific PRs and approvers.
KSI-CMT-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: The change-management-procedure.md document exists in CodeCommit, AND the Terraform state file exists in S3 with version history proving sequential, tracked infrastructure changes.
KSI-CMT-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The change management procedure document is missing, or no Terraform state file exists in the mks-states bucket.

KSI-CNA-01.description: Persistently ensure all machine-based information resources are configured to limit inbound and outbound network traffic.
KSI-CNA-01.justification: Validates Network Traffic Limiting. Checks for: 1) The 'default' Security Group (Baseline Hygiene - should be empty), 2) Active Security Groups (Specific Ingress/Egress Rules), and 3) Network ACLs (Subnet Boundary Control).

cli_commands.note: BASELINE: Validate that the 'default' VPC Security Group restricts all traffic (Best practice is to leave this empty/unused).
cli_commands.expected_output: Default security group must exist for baseline validation

cli_commands.note: ACTIVE CONTROLS: Validate a sample of active Security Groups to verify specific port restrictions (Limit Inbound).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least one TCP-permitting security group must exist

cli_commands.note: BOUNDARY: Validate Network ACLs which provide a stateless traffic filter at the subnet level.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least one network ACL must exist for subnet-level filtering

KSI-CNA-01.implementation_summary: Network traffic limited via Security Groups (default SG empty, specific allow-lists only) and NACLs at subnet boundaries. All ingress flows through ALB.
KSI-CNA-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: The default VPC security group exists with no inbound or outbound rules (empty/unused as best practice), at least one TCP-permitting security group exists with specific port restrictions (not 0.0.0.0/0 on all ports), AND at least one network ACL provides subnet-level filtering.
KSI-CNA-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The default security group has active inbound/outbound rules, security groups allow unrestricted access (0.0.0.0/0 on all ports), or no network ACLs exist.

KSI-CNA-02.description: Persistently ensure machine-based information resources have a minimal attack surface and that lateral movement is minimized if compromised.
KSI-CNA-02.justification: Validates Network Segmentation and Isolation. Checks for: 1) Private Subnets (Attack Surface Reduction), 2) Security Group References (Prevention of Lateral Movement by binding rules to logical groups, not IPs), and 3) Private Database Placement.

cli_commands.note: ATTACK SURFACE: Validate the existence of Private Subnets (where Public IP mapping is disabled).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 subnet must exist

cli_commands.note: LATERAL MOVEMENT: Validate Security Groups that allow access ONLY from other specific Security Groups (Tiered Security), rather than broad IP ranges.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 security group must exist

cli_commands.note: ISOLATION: Validate that RDS Databases are configured as 'PubliclyAccessible: false' and reside in defined subnets.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 RDS database instance must exist

KSI-CNA-02.implementation_summary: Attack surface minimized via private subnets for compute/database, Security Group references (no IP-based rules), and RDS PubliclyAccessible=false.
KSI-CNA-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one private subnet exists (MapPublicIpOnLaunch=false), security groups reference other security groups (tiered access, not broad IP ranges), AND all RDS instances have PubliclyAccessible=false.
KSI-CNA-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No private subnets exist, security groups use only broad IP-based rules instead of security group references, or any RDS instance has PubliclyAccessible=true.

KSI-CNA-03.description: Use logical networking and related capabilities to enforce traffic flow controls.
KSI-CNA-03.justification: Validates Logical Traffic Enforcement. Checks for: 1) VPC Endpoints (Enforces private connectivity to AWS services), 2) NAT Gateways (Enforces centralized egress), and 3) Load Balancer Schemes (Enforces defined entry points).

cli_commands.note: PRIVATE FLOW: Validate existence of VPC Endpoints, ensuring traffic to services like S3/DynamoDB bypasses the public internet.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 VPC endpoint must exist

cli_commands.note: EGRESS CONTROL: Validate active NAT Gateways. This proves instances in private subnets route outbound traffic centrally, rather than having individual Public IPs.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 NAT gateway must exist

cli_commands.note: INGRESS CONTROL: Validate Load Balancers. The 'Scheme' (internet-facing vs internal) proves that ingress traffic is logically separated and managed.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 load balancer must exist

KSI-CNA-03.implementation_summary: Traffic flow enforced via VPC Endpoints (S3, DynamoDB), NAT Gateway for egress, and ALB for ingress. No direct internet access from compute tier.
KSI-CNA-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one VPC endpoint exists (enforcing private connectivity to AWS services like S3/DynamoDB), at least one NAT gateway is available (centralized egress for private subnets), AND at least one load balancer manages ingress traffic.
KSI-CNA-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No VPC endpoints exist (traffic to AWS services traverses public internet), no NAT gateway is available, or no load balancer exists for ingress management.

KSI-CNA-04.description: Use immutable infrastructure with strictly defined functionality and privileges by default.
KSI-CNA-04.justification: Validates Immutable EC2 Infrastructure & Least Privilege. Checks for: 1) Terraform State (Proof of immutable IaC management), 2) Security Groups (Strict network functionality), and 3) Custom IAM Roles (Strictly defined privileges, excluding default AWS service roles).

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-objects-v2 --bucket mks-states --prefix terraform.tfstate --max-items 1 --output json
cli_commands.note: IMMUTABILITY: List Terraform State file with versioning metadata, proving version-controlled infrastructure changes.
cli_commands.expected_output: S3 object must exist at specified prefix

cli_commands.note: FUNCTIONALITY: Check for Security Groups allowing 'All Traffic' (-1). Absence of these proves strictly defined network functionality.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 security group must exist

cli_commands.note: PRIVILEGE: Audit Custom IAM Roles. This focuses the scan on roles you created, verifying they follow least-privilege principles.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 custom IAM role must exist

KSI-CNA-04.implementation_summary: Immutable infrastructure via Terraform. EC2 instances replaced (not modified) on updates. IAM roles follow least-privilege with no wildcard permissions.
KSI-CNA-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: A Terraform state file exists in S3 (proving IaC management), no security groups allow unrestricted 'All Traffic' protocol (-1) to 0.0.0.0/0, AND at least one custom IAM role exists following least-privilege principles (excluding AWS service-linked roles).
KSI-CNA-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No Terraform state exists (infrastructure not immutable), any security group allows 'All Traffic' to 0.0.0.0/0, or no custom IAM roles exist.

KSI-CNA-05.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of protection against denial of service attacks and other unwanted activity.
KSI-CNA-05.justification: Validates DDoS & Spoofing Defense. Checks for: 1) Regional WAF (Layer 7 Defense), 2) Load Balancers (Layer 3/4 Defense via AWS Shield Standard), and 3) Validated Email Identities (Spoofing protection via SES/DKIM).

cli_commands.note: DDOS LAYER 7: Validate existence of Web Application Firewalls (WAF) to block malicious requests.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 WAF web ACL must exist

cli_commands.command: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query "LoadBalancers[].{ARN:LoadBalancerArn, DNS:DNSName, Shield: 'Enabled (Standard)'}" --output json
cli_commands.note: DDOS LAYER 3/4: Validate Load Balancers. All ALBs act as the perimeter and automatically benefit from AWS Shield Standard.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 load balancer must exist

cli_commands.note: DNS RESILIENCE: Validate Route53 usage (AWS AnyCast network provides DNS flood protection).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Route53 hosted zone must exist

cli_commands.note: SPOOFING: Validate SES Identities. If email is sent, this checks for authorized identities (DKIM/SPF managed).
cli_commands.expected_output: SES identities should exist if email is used (validates DKIM/SPF anti-spoofing)

KSI-CNA-05.implementation_summary: DDoS protection via AWS Shield Standard (automatic on ALB), WAFv2 Web ACLs, and Route53 anycast. SES handles email with DKIM/SPF validation.
KSI-CNA-05.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one WAFv2 Web ACL exists (Layer 7 protection), at least one load balancer with Shield Standard provides Layer 3/4 protection, at least one Route53 hosted zone exists (DNS resilience), AND if SES is used, verified identities exist with DKIM/SPF.
KSI-CNA-05.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No WAF Web ACLs exist, no load balancers provide Shield protection, or no Route53 hosted zones exist for DNS resilience.

KSI-CNA-06.description: Appropriately optimize machine-based information resources for high availability and rapid recovery.
KSI-CNA-06.justification: Validates Multi-AZ High Availability. Checks for: 1) Load Balancers spanning multiple AZs (Network HA), 2) RDS instances with Multi-AZ enabled (Database HA), and 3) Active AWS Backup Plans (Recovery Capability).

cli_commands.note: NETWORK HA: Validate that Load Balancers are configured across multiple Availability Zones.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 load balancer must exist

cli_commands.note: DATABASE HA: Validate that critical RDS instances have Multi-AZ enabled for automatic failover.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 RDS database instance must exist

cli_commands.note: RECOVERY: Validate existence of Backup Plans to support rapid data recovery.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 backup plan must exist

KSI-CNA-06.implementation_summary: High availability via Multi-AZ ALB, Multi-AZ RDS, and AWS Backup plans with 24-hour RPO. RTO target: 4 hours for full recovery.
KSI-CNA-06.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one load balancer spans multiple Availability Zones, at least one RDS instance has MultiAZ=true for automatic failover, AND at least one AWS Backup plan exists for data recovery.
KSI-CNA-06.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Load balancers are in a single AZ, no RDS instance has MultiAZ enabled, or no backup plans exist for recovery.

KSI-CNA-07.description: Persistently ensure cloud-native machine-based information resources are implemented based on the host providers best practices and documented guidance.
KSI-CNA-07.justification: Validates cloud-native maturity by comparing the ratio of legacy infrastructure (EC2) versus modern managed services (Lambda, RDS, API Gateway, etc).

cli_commands.note: BASELINE: Measure legacy compute footprint (Denominator) to compare against managed service adoption.
cli_commands.expected_output: EC2 instances enumerated as legacy compute baseline

cli_commands.note: ACCELERATOR: Validate adoption of serverless compute (High Impact).
cli_commands.expected_output: Lambda functions should exist demonstrating serverless adoption

cli_commands.note: ACCELERATOR: Validate use of fully managed databases vs self-hosted DBs.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 RDS instance should exist as managed database

cli_commands.note: ACCELERATOR: Validate use of managed API hosting infrastructure.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 API Gateway REST API should exist

cli_commands.note: ACCELERATOR: Validate use of managed application networking.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 load balancer should exist as managed networking

cli_commands.note: ACCELERATOR: Validate use of dynamic scaling/statelessness vs static servers.
cli_commands.expected_output: Auto Scaling groups indicate dynamic scaling capability

KSI-CNA-07.implementation_summary: Cloud-native architecture: 21 Lambda functions, 1 managed RDS, API Gateway. Legacy EC2 (5 instances) hardened with IMDSv2, no public IPs, VPC-only Lambdas.
KSI-CNA-07.pass_criteria: PASS if: The ratio of managed services (Lambda functions, RDS, API Gateway, ALB, ASG) to legacy EC2 instances demonstrates cloud-native adoption. At least 2 categories of managed services must be in use alongside EC2.
KSI-CNA-07.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Only EC2 instances exist with no managed service adoption (no Lambda, no managed DB, no API Gateway), indicating failure to follow cloud-native best practices.

KSI-CNA-08.description: Use automated services to persistently assess the security posture of all machine-based information resources and automatically enforce their intended operational state.
KSI-CNA-08.justification: Validates that the 'Immune System' of the cloud is active by checking for enabled Security Hub Standards, GuardDuty Detectors, and Config Recorders.

cli_commands.note: Validate that Security Hub is active and enforcing specific compliance standards (CIS, PCI, etc).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Security Hub standard must exist

cli_commands.note: Validate that GuardDuty is enabled (Detector ID exists) for automated threat detection.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 GuardDuty detector must exist

cli_commands.note: Validate that AWS Config is actively recording resource changes for persistent assessment.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Config recorder must exist

cli_commands.note: Validate that Amazon Inspector is actively scanning resources (EC2/ECR/Lambda).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Inspector-covered resource must exist

KSI-CNA-08.implementation_summary: Security posture assessed by SecurityHub (CIS/FedRAMP standards), GuardDuty (threat detection), AWS Config (compliance rules), and Inspector (vulnerability scanning).
KSI-CNA-08.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one Security Hub compliance standard is enabled (CIS, FedRAMP, PCI), at least one GuardDuty detector is active, at least one AWS Config recorder exists, AND at least one resource is covered by Amazon Inspector scanning.
KSI-CNA-08.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No Security Hub standards are enabled, no GuardDuty detector exists, no Config recorder is configured, or no Inspector coverage exists.

KSI-IAM-01.description: Enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) using methods that are difficult to intercept or impersonate (phishing-resistant MFA) for all user authentication.
KSI-IAM-01.justification: Validates MFA compliance by filtering for users who actively log in via password (Humans) and verifying Identity Center usage. Excludes API-only service accounts.

cli_commands.note: FILTERED: Retrieve only IAM Users who have active password usage (Humans).
cli_commands.expected_output: All IAM users with PasswordLastUsed must have MFA enabled

cli_commands.note: Verify that AWS Identity Center is the primary identity platform.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Identity Center instance must exist

cli_commands.note: Get list of all Virtual MFA devices to validate protection (replaces list-mfa-devices which requires user).
cli_commands.expected_output: Virtual MFA devices should be assigned to all human users

KSI-IAM-01.implementation_summary: MFA enforced for all human users via AWS Identity Center with FIDO2/WebAuthn support. Break-glass IAM users require hardware tokens.
KSI-IAM-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: All IAM users with active password usage (console access) have MFA enabled, AWS Identity Center is configured as the primary identity platform, AND virtual MFA devices are inventoried with no unprotected human accounts.
KSI-IAM-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Any IAM user with console access lacks MFA, Identity Center is not configured, or human accounts exist without any MFA device assigned.

KSI-IAM-02.description: Use secure passwordless methods for user authentication and authorization when feasible, otherwise enforce strong passwords with MFA.
KSI-IAM-02.justification: Validates the authentication hierarchy: Checks for Identity Center (Best) and SAML Providers (Good) first. If those are absent, it validates the IAM Password Policy (Fallback) to ensure strong controls are in place.

cli_commands.note: MODERN: Validate presence of AWS Identity Center (Single Sign-On).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Identity Center instance must exist

cli_commands.note: LEGACY/FEDERATED: Validate presence of external Identity Providers (Okta, Azure AD).
cli_commands.expected_output: SAML providers may exist for federated identity (Okta, Azure AD)

cli_commands.command: aws iam get-account-password-policy --output json || echo '{"PasswordPolicy": null}'
cli_commands.note: FALLBACK: Validate that if passwords ARE used, the policy enforces complexity and rotation.
cli_commands.expected_output: Password policy must enforce MinimumPasswordLength >= 14, RequireSymbols, RequireNumbers, MaxPasswordAge <= 90

KSI-IAM-02.implementation_summary: Primary authentication via AWS Identity Center (SSO). Fallback IAM password policy enforces 14+ chars, complexity, 90-day rotation.
KSI-IAM-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: AWS Identity Center (SSO) is configured as the primary authentication method. If SAML providers exist, they provide federated access. If IAM passwords are used as fallback, the password policy enforces minimum 14 characters, complexity requirements, and 90-day rotation.
KSI-IAM-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No Identity Center instance exists AND no SAML providers are configured (no centralized auth), or the IAM password policy allows passwords shorter than 14 characters or lacks complexity/rotation requirements.

KSI-IAM-03.description: Enforce appropriately secure authentication methods for non-user accounts and services.
KSI-IAM-03.justification: Validates the security of machine identities. Filters out AWS-managed roles to focus strictly on Customer Roles (Trust Policies) and Service Accounts (Access Keys).

cli_commands.note: Validate Trust Policies for CUSTOMER managed roles only.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 custom IAM role must exist

cli_commands.note: Validate Service Accounts (Bots) that do not have console access (should rely on keys/roles).
cli_commands.expected_output: Service accounts (no PasswordLastUsed) should use roles, not long-lived keys

cli_commands.command: aws iam get-credential-report --output text > /dev/null && aws iam get-credential-report --output json || echo '{"Content": "Permission Denied"}'
cli_commands.note: Deep inspection of access key rotation status for service accounts.
cli_commands.expected_output: Credential report must show access keys rotated within 90 days

KSI-IAM-03.implementation_summary: Service accounts use IAM Roles with no long-lived credentials. Access keys (where required) rotate every 90 days per credential report monitoring.
KSI-IAM-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: All custom IAM roles have trust policies scoped to specific services or accounts (no wildcard principals), service accounts (users without console access) rely on roles or have access keys rotated within 90 days per the credential report.
KSI-IAM-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Any custom role has a wildcard (*) principal in its trust policy, or the credential report shows access keys older than 90 days for service accounts.

KSI-IAM-04.description: Require a centralized identity management system.
KSI-IAM-04.justification: Validates the presence of the Centralized Identity Provider (Identity Center) and audits the 'Debt' of decentralized local IAM users.

cli_commands.note: PRIMARY: Validate that AWS Identity Center (SSO) is configured and running.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Identity Center instance must exist

cli_commands.note: AUDIT: List human users who are bypassing the Centralized System (should be empty or minimal).
cli_commands.expected_output: Zero or minimal IAM users with PasswordLastUsed (target: 0 human IAM users)

KSI-IAM-04.implementation_summary: Centralized identity via AWS Identity Center. Legacy IAM users audited and minimized (target: 0 human IAM users).
KSI-IAM-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: AWS Identity Center (SSO) is configured and active, AND the number of IAM users with direct console access (bypassing centralized identity) is zero or minimal (break-glass only).
KSI-IAM-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No Identity Center instance exists, or multiple IAM users have active console passwords indicating widespread bypass of centralized identity management.

KSI-IAM-05.description: Persistently ensure that identity and access management employs measures to ensure each user or device can only access the resources they need.
KSI-IAM-05.justification: Validates that the authentication flow prevents phishing by enforcing FIDO2/WebAuthn at the Identity Provider level (SSO) and ensuring break-glass users utilize hardware tokens.

cli_commands.note: PRIMARY: Validate Identity Center configuration for WebAuthn/FIDO2 enforcement.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Identity Center instance must exist

cli_commands.note: AUDIT: Inventory MFA devices to identify non-FIDO compliant virtual tokens.
cli_commands.expected_output: Virtual MFA devices inventoried; FIDO2/hardware tokens preferred over TOTP

cli_commands.command: aws iam get-account-password-policy --output json || echo '{"PasswordPolicy": null}'
cli_commands.note: Validate that hard-fail measures are in place if MFA is missing.
cli_commands.expected_output: Password policy must exist as hard-fail backstop (MinimumPasswordLength >= 14)

KSI-IAM-05.implementation_summary: Phishing-resistant MFA enforced via AWS Identity Center with FIDO2/WebAuthn support. Virtual MFA devices audited to identify non-compliant tokens. IAM password policy provides hard-fail backstop if MFA is missing.
KSI-IAM-05.pass_criteria: PASS if: AWS Identity Center is configured with FIDO2/WebAuthn support, all MFA devices are inventoried (preference for hardware/FIDO tokens over virtual), AND the IAM password policy provides a hard-fail backstop if MFA is missing.
KSI-IAM-05.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Identity Center is not configured, MFA device inventory shows unprotected accounts, or no password policy exists as fallback protection.

KSI-IAM-06.description: Automatically disable or otherwise secure accounts with privileged access in response to suspicious activity
KSI-IAM-06.justification: Validates the 'Automated Response' chain: Checks if GuardDuty is watching (Detectors) AND if EventBridge has rules listening to GuardDuty to trigger automated remediation.

cli_commands.note: SENSOR: Validate that the Threat Detection sensor is active.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 GuardDuty detector must exist

cli_commands.note: BRAIN: Validate existence of EventBridge rules that listen for GuardDuty signals (evidence of automated response logic).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 EventBridge rule monitoring IAM events must exist

KSI-IAM-06.implementation_summary: Automated response to suspicious activity via GuardDuty → EventBridge → Lambda chain. High-severity findings trigger automatic credential rotation.
KSI-IAM-06.pass_criteria: PASS if: The access-review-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit defining the access review process, AND at least one EventBridge rule exists that monitors IAM events (CreateUser, AttachRolePolicy, etc.) for automated access review triggers.
KSI-IAM-06.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The access review policy document is missing from CodeCommit, or no EventBridge rules exist to monitor IAM events (no automated access review capability).

KSI-IAM-07.description: Securely manage the lifecycle and privileges of all accounts, roles, and groups, using automation.
KSI-IAM-07.justification: Validates proper lifecycle management by auditing credential rotation. Instead of listing static roles, it scans the Credential Report for stale passwords (>90 days) and unused access keys.

cli_commands.command: aws iam generate-credential-report > /dev/null && aws iam get-credential-report --output text | base64 --decode || echo 'Permission Denied'
cli_commands.note: PRIMARY: Retrieve the full Credential Report to analyze password and key rotation ages.
cli_commands.expected_output: Credential report must show password and access key rotation within 90 days

cli_commands.note: AUDIT: Identify potential 'Zombie Roles' that are older than a specific threshold (adjust date as needed).
cli_commands.expected_output: No IAM roles older than 365 days without recent usage should exist

KSI-IAM-07.implementation_summary: Account lifecycle managed via credential report monitoring. Stale credentials (>90 days unused) flagged for removal. Termination process documented.
KSI-IAM-07.pass_criteria: PASS if: The IAM credential report shows all passwords and access keys are compliant with rotation policies (passwords rotated within 90 days, access keys within 90 days), AND no IAM roles older than 365 days exist without recent usage (no zombie roles).
KSI-IAM-07.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The credential report shows passwords or access keys exceeding rotation thresholds, or IAM roles exist that are older than 365 days with no recent activity (indicating orphaned/zombie roles).

KSI-INR-01.description: Persistently review of the effectiveness of documented incident response procedures.
KSI-INR-01.justification: Validates the existence of Incident Response documentation. Specifically checks for the existence of a 'Governance as Code' repository containing procedures and playbooks.

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit list-repositories --query "repositories[?contains(repositoryName, 'policy') || contains(repositoryName, 'compliance') || contains(repositoryName, 'playbook') || contains(repositoryName, 'security') || contains(repositoryName, 'procedures') || contains(repositoryName, 'runbook')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: Validate existence of the designated Policy/Runbook repository (filters out application code).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CodeCommit repository must exist

KSI-INR-01.implementation_summary: IR procedures documented in CodeCommit (security-governance/procedures/). Effectiveness reviewed via tabletop exercises and actual incident metrics.
KSI-INR-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one GuardDuty detector is active and returning findings, providing automated threat detection for the AWS environment.
KSI-INR-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No GuardDuty detector exists or GuardDuty is disabled, leaving the environment without automated threat detection.

KSI-INR-02.description: Persistently review past incidents for patterns or vulnerabilities.
KSI-INR-02.justification: Validates the existence of centralized incident logging infrastructure. Checks for specific CloudWatch Log Groups and S3 Buckets designated for security/audit data.

cli_commands.command: aws logs describe-log-groups --query "logGroups[?contains(logGroupName, 'security') || contains(logGroupName, 'cloudtrail') || contains(logGroupName, 'audit') || contains(logGroupName, 'incident')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: Validate existence of centralized CloudWatch Log Groups for security events.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CloudWatch log group must exist

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[?contains(Name, 'log') || contains(Name, 'audit') || contains(Name, 'security')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: Validate existence of immutable S3 storage for long-term incident retention.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 matching S3 bucket must exist

KSI-INR-02.implementation_summary: Past incidents logged in CloudWatch Logs and S3 audit buckets. GuardDuty findings provide pattern analysis for recurring threats.
KSI-INR-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: The incident-response-plan.md document exists in CodeCommit defining response procedures, AND GuardDuty is active with at least one detector providing the finding source for incident triggers.
KSI-INR-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The incident response plan document is missing from CodeCommit, or no GuardDuty detector exists to generate security findings.

KSI-INR-03.description: Generate incident after action reports and persistently incorporate lessons learned.
KSI-INR-03.justification: Validates the existence of a 'Lessons Learned' repository. Checks for CodeCommit repositories specifically designated for Post-Incident Analysis (Post-Mortems) or After Action Reports (AAR).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit list-repositories --query "repositories[?contains(repositoryName, 'post-mortem') || contains(repositoryName, 'aar') || contains(repositoryName, 'lessons-learned') || contains(repositoryName, 'incident-reports') || contains(repositoryName, 'reports')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: Validate existence of the designated Post-Mortem/AAR repository.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CodeCommit repository must exist

KSI-INR-03.implementation_summary: After-action reports generated via Lambda function and stored in CodeCommit. Lessons learned incorporated into playbook updates.
KSI-INR-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one GuardDuty detector is active and configured, providing continuous monitoring for post-incident review and lessons learned analysis.
KSI-INR-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No GuardDuty detector exists, preventing post-incident analysis and continuous improvement of response procedures.

KSI-MLA-01.description: Log all activity on all information resources supporting the cloud service offering to a protected audit log.
KSI-MLA-01.justification: Validates the master audit record. Checks for the existence of an active CloudTrail and the security (ACLs/Encryption) of the specific S3 bucket storing those logs.

cli_commands.note: PRIMARY: Validate existence and configuration of the CloudTrail trail.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least one CloudTrail trail must be configured

cli_commands.command: aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name $(aws cloudtrail describe-trails --query 'trailList[0].TrailARN' --output text) --output json
cli_commands.note: STATUS: Confirm CloudTrail is actually 'IsLogging': true. Uses TrailARN to avoid naming conflicts.
cli_commands.expected_output: CloudTrail must be actively logging (IsLogging: true)

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[?contains(Name, 'cloudtrail') || contains(Name, 'audit') || contains(Name, 'logs')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: STORAGE: Validate the specific S3 bucket used for audit log retention.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least one audit log bucket must exist

KSI-MLA-01.implementation_summary: All activity logged to CloudTrail (multi-region, S3 delivery). VPC Flow Logs capture network traffic. Log integrity via S3 Object Lock.
KSI-MLA-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one CloudWatch Log Group exists for centralized logging, CloudTrail is enabled for API audit logging, AND at least one CloudWatch alarm exists to monitor log-derived metrics.
KSI-MLA-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No CloudWatch Log Groups exist, CloudTrail is not enabled, or no CloudWatch alarms are configured for monitoring.

KSI-MLA-02.justification: Validates 'Automated Review'. Instead of expecting human review, checks for CloudWatch Metric Filters (automated pattern matching) and Security Hub Insights (aggregated analysis).

cli_commands.note: PRIMARY: Validate existence of automated log scanning patterns (e.g., 'RootLogin', 'Unauthorized').
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CloudWatch Logs metric filter must exist for automated log scanning

cli_commands.note: SECONDARY: Validate usage of Security Hub for aggregated security trend analysis.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Security Hub insight must exist

cli_commands.command: aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "MetricAlarms[?contains(AlarmName, 'Log') || contains(AlarmName, 'Security') || contains(AlarmName, 'Filter')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: ALERTING: Validate alarms specifically tied to log metric filters.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CloudWatch alarm tied to log metric filters must exist

KSI-MLA-02.implementation_summary: Log review automated via CloudWatch Metric Filters (root login, unauthorized API calls), SecurityHub Insights for trend analysis, and CloudWatch Alarms for threshold-based alerting on failed logins, log delivery failures, retention violations, and security group changes.
KSI-MLA-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one CloudWatch Logs metric filter exists for automated log scanning patterns, at least one CloudWatch Log Group exists for log aggregation, AND at least one CloudWatch alarm is tied to log metric filters for automated alerting.
KSI-MLA-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No metric filters exist (no automated log scanning), no Log Groups exist, or no alarms are configured for log-based alerting.

KSI-MLA-05.description: Persistently evaluate and test the configuration of machine-based information resources, especially infrastructure as code.
KSI-MLA-05.justification: Validates the IaC workflow using Terraform artifacts. Checks for '.tfplan' files (Proof of Evaluation/Testing) and '.tfstate' files (Proof of Configuration Management) in the central state bucket.

cli_commands.command: aws s3 ls s3://mks-states/ --recursive | grep -E '\.tfstate|\.tfplan' || echo 'No State Files Found'
cli_commands.note: PRIMARY: Scan the Terraform State bucket. Presence of '.tfplan' proves pre-deployment evaluation; '.tfstate' proves active configuration management.
cli_commands.expected_output: Terraform state/plan files must exist in the mks-states bucket

cli_commands.note: SOURCE: Validate existence of the Infrastructure-as-Code repository.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CodeCommit repository must exist

KSI-MLA-05.implementation_summary: IaC evaluated via Checkov in CI pipeline. Terraform plans reviewed pre-apply. Config rules detect drift post-deployment.
KSI-MLA-05.pass_criteria: PASS if: The Terraform state bucket contains .tfplan or .tfstate files proving infrastructure change history, AND the sdlc-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit mandating change validation.
KSI-MLA-05.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No Terraform state/plan files exist in the state bucket (no change history), or the SDLC policy is missing from CodeCommit.

KSI-MLA-07.description: Maintain a list of information resources and event types that will be monitored, logged, and audited.
KSI-MLA-07.justification: Validates the Resource Inventory and Logging Strategy. Checks AWS Config (The Resource List) and CloudTrail/Security Log Groups (The Audit Execution).

cli_commands.note: INVENTORY: Validate that AWS Config is recording the 'List of Information Resources'.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Config recorder must exist

cli_commands.note: EVENTS: Validate that CloudTrail is defining the 'Event Types' to be logged.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CloudTrail trail must exist

cli_commands.command: aws logs describe-log-groups --query "logGroups[?contains(logGroupName, 'security') || contains(logGroupName, 'cloudtrail') || contains(logGroupName, 'audit') || contains(logGroupName, 'flow')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: EXECUTION: Validate that the specific Security/Audit logs are actually being captured.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CloudWatch log group must exist

KSI-MLA-07.implementation_summary: Resource inventory maintained by AWS Config (continuous recording of all supported resource types). Event types defined in CloudTrail event selectors (multi-region, global service events). Security log groups capture audit, incident, VPC flow, and compliance data with 365-731 day retention.
KSI-MLA-07.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one CloudTrail trail is actively logging, at least one CloudWatch Log Group exists for log retention, AND the log-management-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit defining retention requirements.
KSI-MLA-07.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No CloudTrail trail is logging, no Log Groups exist for retention, or the log management policy is missing.

KSI-MLA-08.description: Use a least-privileged, role and attribute-based, and just-in-time access authorization model for access to log data.
KSI-MLA-08.justification: Validates the Log Governance Model. Checks for: 1) The Audit Vault (S3), 2) The Encryption Key (KMS), and 3) The Access Policies (IAM) specifically designed for logging.

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[?contains(Name, 'compliance') || contains(Name, 'security') || contains(Name, 'artifacts') || contains(Name, 'audit') || contains(Name, 'logs')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: VAULT: Validate existence of compliance/security buckets.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 matching S3 bucket must exist

cli_commands.command: aws kms list-aliases --query "Aliases[?contains(AliasName, 'aws/s3') || contains(AliasName, 'key') || contains(AliasName, 'encrypt')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: LOCK: Validate existence of encryption keys.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 KMS alias must exist

cli_commands.command: aws iam list-policies --scope Local --query "Policies[?contains(PolicyName, 'ReadOnly') || contains(PolicyName, 'Audit') || contains(PolicyName, 'Log') || contains(PolicyName, 'View')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: PERMIT: Validate existence of access control policies.
cli_commands.expected_output: Non-empty result required for: PERMIT: Validate existence of access control policies.

KSI-MLA-08.implementation_summary: Log access controlled via S3 bucket policies (security team read-only), KMS encryption, and CloudTrail access logging. No broad log access.
KSI-MLA-08.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one CloudWatch alarm exists for automated alerting, at least one SNS topic exists for alert delivery, AND the monitoring-alerting-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit defining alerting thresholds and procedures.
KSI-MLA-08.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No CloudWatch alarms exist, no SNS topics exist for alert delivery, or the monitoring/alerting policy is missing from CodeCommit.

KSI-PIY-01.description: Use authoritative sources to automatically generate real-time inventories of all information resources when needed.
KSI-PIY-01.justification: Validates the active 'Inventory Engine'. Checks for the AWS Config Recorder (which automatically tracks resource changes) and the Delivery Channel (where the inventory is stored).

cli_commands.note: PRIMARY: Validate that the Configuration Recorder is active (The 'Authoritative Source').
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Config recorder must exist

cli_commands.note: STORAGE: Validate that the inventory data is being delivered to a central S3 bucket.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Config delivery channel must exist

cli_commands.command: aws ssm list-inventory-entries --instance-id $(aws ec2 describe-instances --query 'Reservations[0].Instances[0].InstanceId' --output text) --type-name AWS:Application --output json || echo '{"Entries": []}'
cli_commands.note: os-LEVEL: Sample check for SSM Inventory data (software list) on a live instance.
cli_commands.expected_output: SSM inventory data must contain software and OS information for managed instances

KSI-PIY-01.implementation_summary: Real-time inventory via AWS Config with automatic discovery. SSM Inventory provides OS-level software lists. Delivery to S3 for analysis.
KSI-PIY-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: AWS Systems Manager reports managed instances (SSM agent active), an SSM inventory association exists for asset tracking, AND SSM inventory data contains software/OS information for at least one instance.
KSI-PIY-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No SSM-managed instances exist, no inventory association is configured, or no inventory data is available for any instance.

KSI-PIY-03.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of the providers vulnerability disclosure program.
KSI-PIY-03.justification: Validates the existence of the Vulnerability Disclosure Policy (VDP). Checks the governance repository for files matching standard naming conventions (vdp.md, security.txt, disclosure.md).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-folder --repository-name security-governance --folder-path policies/ --output json
cli_commands.note: ARTIFACT: Scan the policies folder for the Disclosure Policy (filters for 'disclosure', 'vdp', or 'security' in filenames).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 policy subfolder must exist

KSI-PIY-03.implementation_summary: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy (VDP) published at trust.meridianks.com/security.txt and in CodeCommit governance repo.
KSI-PIY-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: The data-classification-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit defining data handling categories, sensitivity levels, and handling requirements for federal data.
KSI-PIY-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The data classification policy document is missing from the security-governance repository in CodeCommit.

KSI-PIY-04.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of building security and privacy considerations into the Software Development Lifecycle and aligning with CISA Secure By Design principles.
KSI-PIY-04.justification: Validates the Secure SDLC. Checks for the specific 'Secure Software Development' policy in CodeCommit and identifies automated security/testing build projects (filtering out standard app builds).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/secure-software-development.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate existence of the specific Secure SDLC policy document.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.command: aws codebuild list-projects --query "projects[?contains(@, 'security') || contains(@, 'scan') || contains(@, 'test') || contains(@, 'lint') || contains(@, 'check') || contains(@, 'compliance')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: MECHANISM: List only build projects dedicated to security scanning or testing (The Quality Control gates).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 CodeBuild project must exist

KSI-PIY-04.implementation_summary: Secure SDLC enforced via GitHub PR requirements, Checkov scanning, and CodeBuild security projects. CISA Secure by Design principles documented.
