KSI-PIY-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: The compliance S3 bucket enforces server-side encryption (SSE-S3 or SSE-KMS), AND the data-protection-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit defining encryption requirements for data at rest and in transit.
KSI-PIY-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: The S3 bucket lacks encryption configuration, or the data protection policy is missing from CodeCommit.

KSI-PIY-06.description: Persistently review the effectiveness of the organizations investments in achieving security objectives.
KSI-PIY-06.justification: Validates the Security Effectiveness Program. Checks for the specific 'Security Monitoring and Alerting Plan' (Governance) and active Security Hub Standards (Technical Evidence of Investment).

cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the existence of the specific Security Monitoring Plan document.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.note: INVESTMENT: Validate that Security Hub Standards (CIS, PCI, FedRAMP) are enabled (The automated mechanism for monitoring effectiveness).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Security Hub standard must exist

KSI-PIY-06.implementation_summary: Security investment effectiveness measured via SecurityHub scores, GuardDuty finding trends, and KSI pass rates over time.
KSI-PIY-06.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one S3 bucket has lifecycle rules configured for data retention management, AND the data-retention-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit defining retention schedules and disposal procedures.
KSI-PIY-06.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No S3 lifecycle rules exist for data retention, or the data retention policy is missing from CodeCommit.

KSI-PIY-08.description: Persistently review executive support for achieving the organizations security objectives.
KSI-PIY-08.justification: Validates Executive Buy-in via 'Security Investment'. Checks for active, paid enterprise security tools (GuardDuty, Security Hub) that demonstrate financial commitment to the security program.

cli_commands.note: INVESTMENT: Validate that GuardDuty is enabled (Paid Threat Detection Service).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 GuardDuty detector must exist

cli_commands.note: INVESTMENT: Validate that Security Hub is enabled (Paid Compliance Aggregation Service).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Security Hub standard must exist

cli_commands.command: aws budgets describe-budgets --account-id $(aws sts get-caller-identity --query Account --output text) --output json
cli_commands.note: CONTROL: Validate that a Budget exists to track this security spending.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 AWS Budget must exist for security spend tracking

KSI-PIY-08.implementation_summary: Executive support demonstrated via paid security tools (GuardDuty, SecurityHub, Inspector) and dedicated security budget tracked in AWS Budgets.
KSI-PIY-08.pass_criteria: PASS if: A dedicated security-spend S3 bucket or prefix exists, the security-budget-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit, AND at least one AWS Budget exists to track and limit security-related spending.
KSI-PIY-08.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No security spend tracking exists, the security budget policy is missing, or no AWS Budget is configured to monitor security expenditures.

KSI-RPL-01.description: Persistently review desired Recovery Time Objectives (RTO) and Recovery Point Objectives (RPO).
KSI-RPL-01.justification: Validates the Disaster Recovery Strategy. Checks for the 'Disaster Recovery Plan' (Governance) where RTO/RPO are defined, and active AWS Backup Plans (Technical) which enforce the RPO.

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-folder --repository-name security-governance --folder-path policies/ --query "files[?contains(absolutePath, 'recovery') || contains(absolutePath, 'disaster') || contains(absolutePath, 'continuity') || contains(absolutePath, 'bcp')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate existence of the Disaster Recovery / Business Continuity Plan.
cli_commands.expected_output: Repository content must exist

cli_commands.note: TECHNICAL: Validate existence of AWS Backup Plans (proof that RPO is being enforced via automated schedules).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 backup plan must exist

KSI-RPL-01.implementation_summary: RTO/RPO defined in disaster-recovery-plan.md: RPO=24hrs, RTO=4hrs. AWS Backup enforces daily backups with 30-day retention.
KSI-RPL-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one AWS Backup vault exists for recovery point storage, AND at least one backup plan is configured with defined backup rules and schedules.
KSI-RPL-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No backup vaults exist (no recovery point storage), or no backup plans are configured.

KSI-RPL-02.description: Persistently review the alignment of recovery plans with defined recovery objectives.
KSI-RPL-02.justification: Validates the active execution of the Recovery Plan. Checks for the Governance Document (The Plan) and successful AWS Backup Jobs (The Maintenance/Execution).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/disaster-recovery-plan.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate existence of the defined Recovery Plan.
cli_commands.expected_output: Recovery plan governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.note: EXECUTION: Validate that backup jobs are successfully completing (Proof of Maintenance).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 completed backup job must exist proving recovery capability

KSI-RPL-02.implementation_summary: Recovery plan alignment validated via successful backup job completion (203 jobs tracked). Failed jobs trigger alerts for review.
KSI-RPL-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: The disaster-recovery-plan.md or equivalent recovery plan exists in CodeCommit, AND AWS Backup reports at least one completed backup job proving recovery capability.
KSI-RPL-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No recovery plan document exists in CodeCommit, or no completed backup jobs exist (recovery capability unproven).

KSI-RPL-03.description: Persistently review the alignment of machine-based information resource backups with defined recovery objectives.
KSI-RPL-03.justification: Validates DR Testing. Checks for technical evidence of 'Restore Jobs' (Active Testing) and the formal 'DR Test Report' (Governance Evidence).

cli_commands.note: TECHNICAL: Validate that Restore Jobs have been successfully executed (Proof of Testing).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 completed restore job must exist proving recovery testing

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-folder --repository-name security-governance --folder-path policies/ --query "files[?contains(absolutePath, 'dr-test') || contains(absolutePath, 'restore-report') || contains(absolutePath, 'after-action')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate existence of the Disaster Recovery Test Report.
cli_commands.expected_output: Disaster recovery test plan document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-RPL-03.implementation_summary: Backup alignment validated via quarterly restore tests. Restore jobs documented with duration metrics. Current success rate: 92.9%.
KSI-RPL-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one restore job has been successfully executed (proving recovery testing), AND the disaster-recovery-test-plan.md document exists in CodeCommit defining testing procedures and schedules.
KSI-RPL-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No restore jobs have been executed (recovery never tested), or the DR test plan document is missing from CodeCommit.

KSI-RPL-04.description: Persistently test the capability to recover from incidents and contingencies, including alignment with defined recovery objectives.
KSI-RPL-04.justification: Validates Contingency/Incident Recovery Testing. Checks for the specific 'Incident Response Test Report' (Governance) and evidence of 'Restore Jobs' (Technical capability to recover).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-folder --repository-name security-governance --folder-path policies/ --query "files[?contains(absolutePath, 'incident-response-test') || contains(absolutePath, 'contingency-test') || contains(absolutePath, 'ir-test')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate existence of the Incident Response / Contingency Test Report.
cli_commands.expected_output: Incident response / continuity plan document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.note: TECHNICAL: Validate that Restore Jobs have been executed (Proof of recovery capability).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 restore job must exist proving operational recovery capability

KSI-RPL-04.implementation_summary: Contingency testing via tabletop exercises and actual restore operations. IR test reports stored in CodeCommit with remediation tracking.
KSI-RPL-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: The incident-response-communications.md or equivalent continuity document exists in CodeCommit, AND at least one restore job has been executed proving operational recovery capability during incidents.
KSI-RPL-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No continuity/communications plan exists in CodeCommit, or no restore jobs have been executed to validate recovery during incidents.

KSI-SVC-01.description: Implement improvements based on persistent evaluation of information resources for opportunities to improve security.
KSI-SVC-01.justification: Validates the Continuous Improvement Loop. Checks for 'Evaluators' (Inspector/GuardDuty) and 'Improvers' (SSM Patch Manager) alongside the governance policy.

cli_commands.note: EVALUATION: Validate that Inspector is actively scanning resources (The 'Persistent Evaluation').
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Inspector-covered resource must exist

cli_commands.note: IMPROVEMENT: Validate existence of custom Patch Baselines (The mechanism for 'Implementing Improvements').
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 custom patch baseline must exist (beyond AWS defaults)

cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the policy defining the evaluation and improvement cycle.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-SVC-01.implementation_summary: Continuous improvement via Inspector findings → SSM Patch Manager remediation. Custom patch baselines for security-critical updates.
KSI-SVC-01.pass_criteria: PASS if: AWS SSM Patch Manager reports compliance data for managed instances, at least one custom patch baseline exists (tailored beyond defaults), AND the patch-management-policy.md document exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-SVC-01.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No patch compliance data exists (patching not monitored), no custom patch baselines exist, or the patch management policy is missing.

KSI-SVC-02.description: Encrypt or otherwise secure network traffic.
KSI-SVC-02.justification: Validates Encryption in Transit. Checks for the Encryption Policy (Governance), ACM Certificates (The Keys), and Load Balancer/CloudFront configurations (The Enforcers).

cli_commands.note: KEYS: Validate existence of active, issued TLS certificates.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 ACM certificate must exist

cli_commands.command: aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query "LoadBalancers[].{ARN:LoadBalancerArn, DNS:DNSName, Scheme:Scheme}" --output json
cli_commands.note: GATEKEEPERS: List Load Balancers handling traffic.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 load balancer must exist

cli_commands.note: EDGE: Validate CloudFront distributions (CDN) are configured with certificates.
cli_commands.expected_output: Non-empty response expected

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/encryption-policy.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the Encryption Policy document.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-SVC-02.implementation_summary: Network traffic encryption enforced via ACM-managed TLS certificates on ALB and CloudFront distributions. Encryption policy documented in CodeCommit governance repo. All external-facing endpoints require HTTPS.
KSI-SVC-02.pass_criteria: PASS if: S3 Block Public Access is enabled at the account level (BlockPublicAcls, BlockPublicPolicy, IgnorePublicAcls, RestrictPublicBuckets all true), the compliance bucket blocks public access, S3 access logging is enabled, AND the s3-security-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-SVC-02.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Account-level S3 Block Public Access is not fully enabled, the compliance bucket allows public access, access logging is disabled, or the S3 security policy is missing.

KSI-SVC-04.description: Manage configuration of machine-based information resources using automation.
KSI-SVC-04.justification: Validates Automated Configuration via Terraform. Checks for the Terraform State Bucket (The Configuration Source), the State Lock Table (Concurrency Control), and the Governance Policy.

cli_commands.command: aws s3api list-buckets --query "Buckets[?contains(Name, 'terraform') || contains(Name, 'state') || contains(Name, 'tfstate')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: IAC STATE: Validate existence of the Terraform State S3 bucket (The Proof of Automation).
cli_commands.expected_output: Terraform state S3 bucket must exist with versioning enabled

cli_commands.command: aws dynamodb list-tables --query "TableNames[?contains(@, 'terraform') || contains(@, 'lock') || contains(@, 'state')]" --output json
cli_commands.note: CONCURRENCY: Validate existence of the Terraform State Lock table.
cli_commands.expected_output: DynamoDB lock table for Terraform state must exist

cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the Configuration Management Policy document.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-SVC-04.implementation_summary: Configuration management automated via Terraform with state stored in S3 (mks-states bucket) and DynamoDB state locking for concurrency control. Configuration management policy in CodeCommit mandates IaC-only changes.
KSI-SVC-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: A Terraform state S3 bucket exists with versioning enabled, a DynamoDB lock table exists for state locking, AND at least one running EC2 instance is managed via Terraform.
KSI-SVC-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No Terraform state bucket exists, no DynamoDB lock table exists for concurrency control, or no managed instances can be verified.

KSI-SVC-05.description: Use cryptographic methods to validate the integrity of machine-based information resources.
KSI-SVC-05.justification: Validates Cryptographic Integrity for EC2 Environments. Checks for: 1) CloudTrail Log File Validation (Cryptographic proof of audit logs), 2) S3 Versioning (Integrity of build artifacts/backups), and 3) SSM Session Manager (Cryptographically authenticated/encrypted console access).

cli_commands.note: LOGS: Validate that CloudTrail Log File Validation is enabled (Cryptographic hashing of audit trails).
cli_commands.expected_output: CloudTrail LogFileValidationEnabled must be true

cli_commands.command: aws s3api get-bucket-versioning --bucket $(aws cloudtrail describe-trails --query 'trailList[0].S3BucketName' --output text) --output json
cli_commands.note: STORAGE: Validate that the Audit/Artifact bucket has Versioning enabled (Prevents overwrite tampering).
cli_commands.expected_output: Versioning must be enabled

cli_commands.command: aws ssm describe-instance-information --query "InstanceInformationList[].{ID:InstanceId, PingStatus:PingStatus, AgentVersion:AgentVersion}" --output json
cli_commands.note: SESSIONS: Validate active SSM Agents, which enforce TLS-encrypted, IAM-signed channels for session integrity (Replacing insecure SSH).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 SSM-managed instance must exist

KSI-SVC-05.implementation_summary: Cryptographic integrity validated via CloudTrail Log File Validation (digest hashing of audit trails), S3 bucket versioning on audit/artifact storage (tamper prevention), and SSM Session Manager for TLS-encrypted, IAM-signed instance access replacing SSH.
KSI-SVC-05.pass_criteria: PASS if: CloudTrail log file validation is enabled (LogFileValidationEnabled=true) ensuring tamper-proof logs, S3 server access logging is enabled on the compliance bucket, AND the audit-logging-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-SVC-05.fail_criteria: FAIL if: CloudTrail log file validation is disabled (logs can be tampered with), S3 access logging is not enabled, or the audit logging policy is missing.

KSI-SVC-06.description: Automate management, protection, and regular rotation of digital keys, certificates, and other secrets.
KSI-SVC-06.justification: Validates Automated Secret Lifecycle. Checks for: 1) KMS Keys (Encryption Foundation), 2) Secrets Manager (Automated Rotation of credentials), and 3) ACM Certificates (Automated Renewal of TLS identities).

cli_commands.note: KEYS: Validate existence of KMS Customer Managed Keys (CMKs) for centralized management.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 KMS key must exist

cli_commands.note: ROTATION: Validate Secrets Manager entries (Look for 'RotationEnabled': true in the output).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 secret in Secrets Manager must have RotationEnabled=true

cli_commands.note: CERTIFICATES: Validate active ACM Certificates (Proof of managed renewal).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 ACM certificate must exist

KSI-SVC-06.implementation_summary: Cryptographic operations use AWS KMS with automatic key rotation (annual). Certificate management via ACM with auto-renewal.
KSI-SVC-06.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one KMS key exists for secrets encryption, Secrets Manager contains at least one secret with rotation configured (RotationEnabled=true), AND the secrets-management-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-SVC-06.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No KMS keys exist, no secrets are stored in Secrets Manager or rotation is not enabled, or the secrets management policy is missing.

KSI-SVC-08.description: Persistently review plans, procedures, and the state of information resources after making changes to limit and remove unwanted residual elements that would likely negatively affect the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of federal customer data.
KSI-SVC-08.justification: Validates Data Sanitization and Destruction Capabilities. Checks for S3 Lifecycle Policies (Automated removal of residual data), KMS Keys (Capability for crypto-shredding backups), and the Data Sanitization Policy.

cli_commands.command: aws s3api get-bucket-lifecycle-configuration --bucket $(aws s3api list-buckets --query 'Buckets[0].Name' --output text) --output json || echo '{"Rules": []}'
cli_commands.note: AUTOMATION: Validate S3 Lifecycle Rules that automatically expire/delete temporary or residual data.
cli_commands.expected_output: S3 lifecycle rules must exist for automated data tiering/expiration

cli_commands.note: CAPABILITY: Validate existence of KMS keys. Deleting a key is the industry-standard method for 'Crypto-Shredding' data from immutable backups.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 KMS key must exist

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/data-sanitization-policy.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the Data Sanitization Policy which defines the procedure for destroying customer data.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

KSI-SVC-08.implementation_summary: Residual data removal automated via S3 Lifecycle Policies (automatic expiration of temporary data), KMS key management (enabling crypto-shredding of encrypted backups), and a formal Data Sanitization Policy defining destruction procedures.
KSI-SVC-08.pass_criteria: PASS if: S3 lifecycle rules exist on the compliance bucket for automated data tiering/expiration, S3 versioning is enabled on the compliance bucket, AND the data-lifecycle-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-SVC-08.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No S3 lifecycle rules exist (no automated data management), versioning is disabled on the compliance bucket, or the data lifecycle policy is missing.

KSI-SVC-09.description: Persistently validate the authenticity and integrity of communications between machine-based information resources using automation.
KSI-SVC-09.justification: Validates Communication Integrity. Checks for Active TLS Listeners (Enforcement of Encryption) and ACM Certificates (Validation of Authenticity/Identity).

cli_commands.note: ENFORCERS: Identify active Load Balancers handling traffic.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 load balancer must exist

cli_commands.command: for arn in $(aws elbv2 describe-load-balancers --query 'LoadBalancers[].LoadBalancerArn' --output text 2>/dev/null || echo ''); do aws elbv2 describe-listeners --load-balancer-arn "$arn" --query "Listeners[?Protocol=='HTTPS' || Protocol=='TLS'].{Port:Port, SslPolicy:SslPolicy, Certificates:Certificates}" --output json; done
cli_commands.note: VALIDATION: Verify that listeners are enforcing HTTPS/TLS with specific SSL Policies (authenticity/integrity checks).
cli_commands.expected_output: Load balancer listeners must enforce HTTPS with TLS 1.2+ SSL policy

cli_commands.note: IDENTITY: Validate existence of issued ACM certificates used to prove authenticity.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 ACM certificate must exist

KSI-SVC-09.implementation_summary: Communication authenticity and integrity validated via HTTPS/TLS enforcement on ALB listeners with specific SSL policies (TLS 1.2+), and ACM-issued certificates proving endpoint identity. All load balancer traffic encrypted in transit.
KSI-SVC-09.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one ACM certificate exists for TLS termination, load balancer listeners enforce HTTPS/TLS with a secure SSL policy (TLS 1.2+), AND the transport-security-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-SVC-09.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No ACM certificates exist, load balancer listeners allow HTTP or use SSL policies below TLS 1.2, or the transport security policy is missing.

KSI-SVC-10.description: Remove unwanted federal customer data promptly when requested by an agency in alignment with customer agreements, including from backups if appropriate.
KSI-SVC-10.justification: Validates Data Removal Capabilities. Checks for: 1) The Sanitization Policy (SLA for removal), 2) Backup Plans (Automated Retention/Expiration), and 3) KMS Keys (Immediate Destruction via Crypto-Shredding).

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-file --repository-name security-governance --file-path policies/data-sanitization-policy.md --output json
cli_commands.note: GOVERNANCE: Validate the Data Sanitization Policy (defines the 'Promptly' SLA and procedure).
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.note: RETENTION: Validate Backup Plans to ensure automated expiration rules are in place (Data doesn't live forever).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 backup plan must exist

cli_commands.note: DESTRUCTION: Validate KMS Keys (The mechanism for 'Crypto-Shredding' data from immutable backups).
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 KMS key must exist

KSI-SVC-10.implementation_summary: Data removal capabilities validated via Data Sanitization Policy (defining promptly SLA and procedures), AWS Backup Plans with automated retention/expiration rules, and KMS keys enabling crypto-shredding of data from immutable backups.
KSI-SVC-10.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one IAM role has an attached permissions boundary, at least one SCP or IAM policy enforces service restrictions, AND the least-privilege-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-SVC-10.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No permissions boundaries are applied to any IAM role, no service restriction policies exist, or the least privilege policy is missing.

KSI-TPR-03.description: Persistently identify, review, and mitigate potential supply chain risks.
KSI-TPR-03.justification: Validates the supply chain risk management policy's existence, its maintenance history, and the existence of a formal approval workflow.

cli_commands.note: Retrieve the supply chain risk management policy from CodeCommit to verify its existence and size.
cli_commands.expected_output: Governance document must exist in CodeCommit

cli_commands.note: Validate that a formal approval rule process is configured for the repository.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 approval rule template must exist

cli_commands.command: aws codecommit get-differences --repository-name security-governance --after-commit-specifier refs/heads/main --output json
cli_commands.note: Check the commit history for an audit trail of recent policy updates.
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 commit difference must exist

KSI-TPR-03.implementation_summary: Supply chain risk management policy maintained in CodeCommit (security-governance repo) with formal approval rule templates and version-controlled commit history providing an audit trail of policy reviews and updates.
KSI-TPR-03.pass_criteria: PASS if: At least one SQA or third-party assessment report exists in the compliance bucket, the third-party-assessment-policy.md exists in CodeCommit, AND the Trust Center API is accessible for report distribution.
KSI-TPR-03.fail_criteria: FAIL if: No assessment reports exist in S3, the third-party assessment policy is missing, or the Trust Center API is unreachable.

KSI-TPR-04.description: Automatically monitor third party software information resources for upstream vulnerabilities using mechanisms that may include contractual notification requirements or active monitoring services.
KSI-TPR-04.justification: Validates comprehensive vulnerability scanning from basic Inspector to enterprise-grade continuous scanning and automated remediation

cli_commands.note: Check Inspector configuration for vulnerability scanning
cli_commands.expected_output: Inspector must be configured

cli_commands.note: Validate OS vulnerability findings from Inspector
cli_commands.expected_output: Inspector OS vulnerability findings must be collected (may be zero if fully patched)

cli_commands.note: Check application vulnerability findings from Inspector
cli_commands.expected_output: Inspector application vulnerability findings must be collected

cli_commands.note: Validate active vulnerability findings requiring remediation
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Inspector finding must exist

cli_commands.note: Check Inspector coverage for EC2 instances
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 Inspector-covered resource must exist

cli_commands.note: Validate SSM agent deployment for vulnerability management
cli_commands.expected_output: At least 1 SSM-managed instance must exist

KSI-TPR-04.implementation_summary: Third-party software vulnerabilities monitored via AWS Inspector with continuous scanning enabled for OS and application components. Active findings tracked for remediation. EC2 coverage validated alongside SSM agent deployment for patch management.
KSI-TPR-04.pass_criteria: PASS if: Amazon Inspector is actively scanning resources with coverage data returned, OS-level and application-level vulnerability findings are being collected, vulnerability counts are enumerated, the VDR dashboard data artifact exists in S3, AND the vulnerability-scanning-policy.md exists in CodeCommit.
KSI-TPR-04.fail_criteria: FAIL if: Inspector has no coverage (not scanning), no vulnerability findings are being collected at OS or application level, the VDR dashboard artifact is missing from S3, or the vulnerability scanning policy is missing.
